title: Schoenus niveus
intro: Schoenus niveus, also known as Snow sedge, is a perennial sedge plant native to Australia, characterized by its white or greenish-white flowers and grass-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. Bioactive constituents of Schoenus niveus include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Snow sedge include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to support immune function.
taxonomy: Schoenus niveus is a species of sedge, which belongs to the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family is part of the larger monocot clade, which also includes other flowering plants such as palms and grasses. Within the Cyperaceae family, Schoenus niveus is classified under the genus Schoenus, a group of sedges that are typically found in wetland environments. The genus Schoenus is further divided into several species, with Schoenus niveus being one of the distinct species within this group. The classification of Schoenus niveus reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations as a sedge species.
